title: Aeschynanthus cuernosensis
intro: Aeschynanthus cuernosensis is a flowering plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aeschynanthus cuernosensis include flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been identified as the primary active compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aeschynanthus cuernosensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, which is often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Aeschynanthus cuernosensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is a tropical plant endemic to the Philippines, characterized by its unique characteristics and growth habits. As a member of the Gesneriaceae family, Aeschynanthus cuernosensis is related to other plants such as Saintpaulia and Sinningia. The Gesneriaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are often referred to as African violets or gesneriads.
